summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering budget true-ups for 2023, a presentation by Goshen Local Schools Superintendent Brian Bailey on a $26 million bond levy for school expansions, acceptance of a police body camera grant, facility appraisal authorization, and fire department promotions. The board also scheduled a special workshop on zoning text amendments and reviewed strong financial performance across township funds with $421,000 in total unencumbered growth.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (Jan 10–23, 2024) – passed
Motion to accept $48,421.20 grant from Ohio Office of Criminal Justice Services for Police Body Worn Cameras Program – passed
Motion to schedule special Board of Trustees workshop for zoning text amendments on Monday, February 12, 2024 at 6 PM – passed
Motion to appropriate $4,500 into ARPA Fund 2272 for facility appraisals – passed
Motion to hire CBIZ Valuation Group for facility appraisals at cost not to exceed $4,500 from ARPA Fund 2272 – passed
Motion to declare surplus and list wall-mount data rack for sale on GovDeals – passed
Motion to move Firefighter Paramedic Doug Englund from part-time to volunteer status effective 1-23-24 – passed
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Chief Myers to National Fallen Firefighters Cast Meeting in Baltimore, MD (March 21-22, 2024) at no cost – passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session at 6:45 PM per ORC 121.22 g1 – passed
Motion to promote Lieutenant Todd Eastet to Captain effective 1-28-2024 – passed
Motion to promote Firefighter Paramedic Nick Messer to Lieutenant effective 1-28-2024 – passed
Motion to adjourn at 7:19 PM – passed
action_items: Chief Myers to follow up on Station 18 architect contract expected by next meeting for construction manager process
Administrator Rose and Chief to follow up on Senate bill related to firehouse financing around the 27th/17th date
Administrator Rose to coordinate zoning text amendment workshop with consultants for February 12, 2024
CBIZ Valuation Group to conduct appraisals of township-owned facilities
Staff to list surplus wall-mount data rack on GovDeals for sale
Administrator Rose to work through obstacles distributing $263,000 Ohio EMA funds to mutual aid partners
Laura and team to continue investing township funds and explore putting more money in the bank
Donna (grants writer) to continue grant research and writing efforts
Celebrate Kyle Houck as Officer of the Year 2023
Get plaque ordered for tornado-surviving flag display
key_amounts: $48,421.20 – grant from Ohio Office of Criminal Justice Services for body worn cameras
$4,500 – cost for CBIZ Valuation Group facility appraisals funded from ARPA
$26 million – Goshen Local Schools bond levy on March ballot at 2.9 mils (potentially 2.68 mils with USDA loan)
$421,000 – total unencumbered growth across all township funds in 2023
$158,000 – total unencumbered increase across all funds
$595,000 – ARPA funds spent on road paving in 2023
$263,000 – received from Ohio EMA for mutual aid partners
$1.7 million – total ARPA funds received over two years
36% – property value increase across Clermont County
notable_topics: Goshen Local Schools $26 million bond levy presentation for March ballot
2023 budget true-up and financial review across all funds
CPAAA community service contributions update ($94,482 since 2011)
Station 18 architect contract and construction manager process update
Clermont County 36% property value increase concerns
New construction windfall tax revenue explanation
USDA loan application approved for school bond at 3.75% interest rate
Fire department promotions: Todd Eastet to Captain, Nick Messer to Lieutenant
Doug Englund status change from part-time to volunteer firefighter
Township investing unencumbered funds for first time in history
Senate bill for firehouse financing legislation update
ARPA fund expenditures review including $832,000 in road paving
attendees: Trustee Hauserman (also referred to as Halderman)
Kyle Houck (Officer of the Year 2023, mentioned)
notes: The flag displayed in the meeting room was the flag that was on the pole when the tornado hit and was found in Marr Park in trees; a plaque is being ordered. The township is investing unencumbered funds for reportedly the first time in Goshen Township history. Trustee Hauserman's name appears to be alternately spelled as Halderman in roll calls. Charles Tassel, a congressional candidate for Ohio's 2nd district, attended and briefly addressed the board. The superintendent noted a USDA loan at 3.75% has been approved pending federal budget settlement, which would reduce the school bond millage from 2.9 to 2.68 mils. Cemetery fund went negative in unencumbered, noted as critical at only $20,000. Executive session was held from approximately 6:45 PM to 7:17 PM to discuss employee promotions.
title: BOT 1-23-24
